entry.title: New people manager report
entry.content: Hello, We are looking to report in SF EC people who have recently become people managers. We want to be careful to not include people whose position is a higher level position to only vacant roles. We want to be able to run this report monthly to identify new managers for training, communications, etc. This should include new hires that start out as people managers from the beginning (easier) as well as people who are promoted into manager roles (harder). Having trouble figuring out how to do this - is there some effective-dated field or report that we can use for this? Or how are other people tracking manager changes? Thank you.

Hello

If you expect any answer from the SAP Community, you should provide more information about the dump you are facing. What is the exception in the dump? Can you download the complete dump in a TXT file and attach it to the incident? What are you trying to do when the dump happens?

Asking the right questions will get you the right answers üôÇ 

Best regards,
Caetano

entry.title: SAP TALK WOMEN CWB 2025: Conex√µes que Transformam
entry.content:
Na ter√ßa-feira, 18 de mar√ßo, aconteceu a primeira edi√ß√£o do SAP Talk Women CWB 2025, um evento pensado para fortalecer a comunidade feminina no universo SAP. O encontro foi realizado no Eva Space Coworking, em Curitiba, e reuniu mulheres incr√≠veis para uma noite de networking, aprendizado e inspira√ß√£o.


Um in√≠cio acolhedor
As portas se abriram √Ýs 18h30, e as participantes foram calorosamente recebidas pelos curadores da SAP Community Curitiba, @SimoneAzevedo  e @TiagoFelipe . Logo na chegada, cada uma recebeu um abra√ßo e uma rosa azul, s√≠mbolo da SAP comunidade CWB e da feminilidade.

Para tornar o momento ainda mais especial, a recep√ß√£o contou com uma mesa caprichada de frios, um welcome drink e uma trilha sonora envolvente, na voz da talentosa Marcela Juk. Durante a primeira hora do evento, as participantes puderam se conectar, trocar experi√™ncias e conhecer novas colegas da comunidade SAP.


Conte√∫do Inspirador
√Äs 19h30, os curadores deram as boas-vindas oficiais, compartilhando a miss√£o da SAP Community e destacando a import√¢ncia de eventos como esse para o crescimento profissional das mulheres no setor. Logo ap√≥s, foi a vez de Fernanda Chaves subir ao palco para conduzir a palestra "Empregabilidade Feminina no Mundo SAP: Conex√µes que Transformam Carreiras".
Com um discurso inspirador, Fernanda trouxe reflex√µes valiosas sobre as oportunidades e desafios que as mulheres enfrentam na √°rea de tecnologia, ressaltando a import√¢ncia do networking e do apoio m√∫tuo para impulsionar carreiras.


Momentos de Relaxamento e Surpresas
Al√©m do conte√∫do enriquecedor, o evento tamb√©m proporcionou um momento de autocuidado com quick massagem, garantindo que as participantes sa√≠ssem renovadas. E para fechar a noite com chave de ouro, cada participante recebeu uma linda ta√ßa personalizada, como lembran√ßa especial desse encontro inspirador. Tamb√©m tivemos um sorteio de brindes incr√≠veis, gentilmente oferecidos pela patrocinadora do evento.

At√© 2026!
O SAP Talk Women CWB 2025 foi um verdadeiro sucesso, reunindo mulheres inspiradoras e criando conex√µes valiosas. A energia do evento foi incr√≠vel, como voc√™ pode ver na foto abaixo!


Confira mais fotos do evento no link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1f4vmhNNTgIHHwslfTandFKaq8qPmT6-9?usp=sharing

Agradecimento especial aos nosso patrocinadores, sem voc√™s nada disso seria poss√≠vel. Nosso muito obrigada!!


E a melhor parte? J√° estamos no calend√°rio anual da SAP Community Curitiba! Se voc√™ perdeu essa edi√ß√£o, n√£o se preocupe‚Äînos vemos em 2026 para mais uma noite de trocas, aprendizado e empoderamento feminino!

#SAPTalkWomenCWB

entry.title: Re: CREATE_DEEP_ENTITY using RAP ( Unmanaged )
entry.content:
Hi Ramya,

This can be achieved by implementing read and rba_entity method.

To send modified response you can read data again into this method using keys ... Or other way is to create local buffer class and use the global internal table to fill all required values in deep_create and then loop on this global table in rba method and fill the result into required format ....

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services instantly redirects to Microsoft login
entry.content:
Hi

We are currently working on implementing authentication through the SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS) in our custom application.

In CIS, I have added all users, assigned the correct roles to each user, and created groups for conditional authentication. We have users that authorize against CIS with a CIS user. We also have users that authorize against CIS through Microsoft Entra ID. These users are added to an application that uses OpenID Connect (OIDC).

Everything is fine until this point; it is now also possible to login into the custom application through CIS.

However, the problem arises when logging out a user that used their Microsoft Entra account to login. When logging in and then logging out again, the next time someone tries to log in instead of showing the CIS login screen. It is now once again showing the Microsoft login screen with no return option to the CIS login screen. This makes it not possible for other users to log into the application on that computer anymore without a Microsoft Entra account.

We have noticed it has to do something with cookies; we notice CIS adds a couple of cookies to the browser. Which if we then manually remove it works again, and the CIS login is once again shown. However, since these are HTTP-only and secure cookies, we cannot remove them with JavaScript.

Is there a way we can log out and remove these cookies automatically so CIS always shows its login screen when logging out?

We are using the following endpoint to logout: https://<Cloud Identity Services domain>/oauth2/logout

Beneath is a video that demonstrates this issue:

entry.title: Email Template - TotalGrossAmount value NOT in available variables
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to create a email template to be used when a billing document is sent by email to our customer.

This template has a bunch of variables that can be used in the template like the date of the billing document, etc.

Among those fields you can find {{TotalNetAmount}} and {{TotalTaxAmount}}, but you the TotalGrossAmount is not available as a variable while this field is one of hte most important of a billing document as it is the amount to be paid...

Does someone knows how it can be added ?

Thank you very much for your help üôÇ

entry.title: Taking SAP Build Guided Experiences to the next level
entry.content:
Table of Content

  • An overview of guided experiences
  • Updated Features and Content
    • Restarting Guided Experiences
    • Multi-instance support in Guided Experiences
    • Collapsible Sections
    • Pre-built content for SAP SuccessFactors
  • Conclusion
  • Additional Read

An overview of guided experiences

In our last blog, we elaborated how Guided Experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Powered by SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation, Guided Experiences can help navigate through complex business processes such as parental leave, benefits enrolment, employee relocation, new hire or supplier onboarding, training and enablement, or browsing and & purchasing electronic equipment. Users can model intuitive stages and stages which can by further enriched with content, documents, forms, UI cards and more. Please see our previous blog to learn more about modelling a Guided Experience directly in SAP Build Process Automation from a Guided Process Artefact, creating stages and steps within each stage , deploying to Work Zone and connecting Guided Process as Wizard template in SAP Build Work Zone. In this blog, we will elaborate on the recently released advanced capabilities like multi-instance support, restarting a guided experience, having collapsible sections and pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors.

Updated Features and Content

Restarting Guided Experiences

One of the key updates is the ability to restart guided experiences. This feature allows users to reset and begin the experience from the start, providing flexibility and ensuring that users can correct any mistakes made during the initial run. The restart feature is also very helpful for easily demoing guided experiences. It allows presenters to quickly reset the process and demonstrate the guided experience multiple times without having to manually undo each step.
Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Restart Button
The restart functionality is implemented as a button within the guided experience cover. When users click this button, they are prompted with a warning message that informs them that all progress will be lost, and the guided experience will start over from the beginning. 

Restart Guided Experience

A warning message is provided as a confirmation step, preventing accidental restarts / data loss and ensuring that users make an informed decision:
Warning message when restarting guided experience

Enable/Disable Setting
To provide further customization, the restart feature can be enabled or disabled per wizard. This setting allows administrators to control whether the restart option is available for specific guided experiences. Depending on the use case, restarting may not be applicable or feasible, in which cases this option can be disabled in the wizard settings.
Setting to enable restart

Multi-instance support in Guided Experiences

Another significant enhancement is the introduction of multi-instance support. This feature enables multiple instances of a guided experience to run simultaneously, allowing users to manage and track various processes concurrently. This is very useful for scenarios where multiple tasks need to be handled in parallel.Multi-instance overview for guided experience
Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Use Cases
Multi-instance support can be applied to various guided experience use cases, including but not limited to:

  • Event Planning - Organizing multiple events simultaneously, each with its own set of tasks and requirements.
  • Purchasing IT Equipment - Managing multiple purchase requests for IT equipment, ensuring that each request is tracked and processed independently.
  • Grievances Requests - Handling multiple grievance requests concurrently, allowing for efficient resolution and tracking of each request.

Managing and Viewing All Instances
To facilitate the management of multiple instances, users can view all active instances in a dedicated interface. This interface provides options to:

  • Add Name - Assign a unique name to each instance for easy identification.
  • Restart Instance - Reset an instance to its initial state, allowing users to start the process over.
  • Cancel Instance - Terminate a given guided experience instance, removing it from the active list.

Multi-instance detail view for guided experience

Enable / Disable Setting
Similar to the restart feature, multi-instance support can be enabled or disabled per guided experience. This setting allows workpage owners to control whether the multi-instance option is available for the specific guided experience. Depending on the use case, running multiple instances may not be applicable or feasible, and this option can be disabled in the wizard settings in such a scenario.

Setting to enable multi-instance

Collapsible Sections

The collapsible sections feature is designed to improve the user interface for guided experiences and freestyle sections alike, providing flexibility for both enduser & content creators working with workpages.


Section Settings
Each workpage section comes with dedicated settings, including the option to make it collapsible. This allows users to personalize different sections based on their preferences and needs. Sections can be set to either expanded or collapsed by default, providing a tailored experience for each scenario. A typical use case is creating an FAQ page with many sections where the collapsed by default structure the workpage content nicely.


Note: this is available for both Work Zone only wizard layouts as well as guided experiences based on Process Automation guided processes.

Freestyle Layout
For freestyle layout sections, it is recommended to include a title or information for the dropdown. The title and information act as a header, making it clear what each section contains when collapsed.

Wizard Layout
In guided experiences, collapsible sections support various scenarios such as starting an instance, continuing an instance, and more. These collapsed sections also include a progress bar to visually indicate the user's progress of the guided experience. This feature is particularly useful for placing guided experiences space-efficiently on workpages without cluttering the interface. Example guided experiences in collapsed view:

Pre-built content for SAP SuccessFactors

We are excited to introduce pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors, designed to streamline HR processes and enhance user experience. This content includes ready-to-use templates for employee onboarding and cross-boarding content packages, which can be easily deployed in SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Build Process Automation.

Content Availability
The pre-built content for Process Automation is available in the SAP Build Lobby store, while the content for Work Zone is provided as dedicated content packages via the Work Zone admin UI. These packages - among many other items - also feature a re-usable, configurable UI Card for item lists, allowing for a more personalized user experience for both users and content creators alike.

Scenarios of Content
There are three scenarios of pre-built content available:

  • Legacy (UI5 component card + workflow) - This scenario includes a single UI5 component card per guided experience combined with a workflow. It is however not recommended for new setups due to its outdated structure, limited flexibility & complex modification approach.
  • Work Zone Wizard - This scenario involves a Work Zone local wizard template, including UI cards. It provides a streamlined and intuitive experience for users, allowing them to navigate through processes with ease.
  • Process Automation version - This scenario is the combined version of Work Zone and Process Automation content. It leverages the strengths of both platforms to provide a comprehensive solution for managing complex processes.

For more detailed instructions on how to leverage this pre-built content, please refer to the following SAP Help guide. In this guide we also provide a detailed comparison table of those three scenarios of guided experience pre-built content.

 

Conclusion

The guided experiences are changing the way businesses can manage complex processes. From creating end-to-end onboarding experience for new employees to the application of parental leave, guided experiences are leveraging various capabilities within SAP Build as a deeply integrated, unified solution experience.

 

Additional Read

To learn more about the topic covered in this blog post, please also refer to the following assets & follow us on SAP community.

  • Related Blog Posts:
    • Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build
    • What‚Äôs new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
    • SAP Build Process Automation - Introducing Environments
  • Learning Journeys & Certification: 
    • Work Zone: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning
    • Process Automation: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation
  • Reference Architectures:
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-sap-build-work-zone
    • https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchDetail/ref-arch-build-process-automation
  • SAP Help guides for more details:
    • Work Zone: How to Create a Wizard
    • Process Automation: Create a Guided Process
  • GitHub Repository for Work Zone Examples: https://github.com/SAP-samples/build-workzone-integration/tree/main/advanced/wizard
  • Video example Navigating Parental Leave with SAP Build Guided Experiences
  • 2024 Devtoberfest Guided Experience recording: https://www.youtube.com/live/q1pmO283Y5c?si=wfNm8eDNJPC0t_k8

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Lessons Learned: Hybrid Development with On-Premise SAP System
entry.content:
Hi @SheldonS , as @MioYasutake mentioned, we're overriding the service configuration. We use as a base the VCAP Services:

"connectivity": [
			{
				"label": "connectivity",
				"provider": null,
				"plan": "lite",
				"name": "myapp-connectivity",
				"tags": [
					"connectivity",
					"conn",
					"connsvc"
				],
				"instance_guid": "1234",
				"instance_name": "myapp-connectivity",
				"binding_guid": "1234",
				"binding_name": null,
				"credentials": {
					"tenantmode": "dedicated",
					"clientid": "sb-1234!b123|connectivity!b8",
					"token_service_domain": "authentication.us20.hana.ondemand.com",
					"credential-type": "binding-secret",
					"token_service_url": "https://myorg.authentication.us20.hana.ondemand.com",
					"xsappname": "clone1234!b123|connectivity!b8",
					"onpremise_proxy_ldap_port": "20001",
					"onpremise_socks5_proxy_port": "20004",
					"clientsecret": "1234=",
					"onpremise_proxy_http_port": "20003",
					"url": "https://myorg.authentication.us20.hana.ondemand.com",
					"onpremise_proxy_host": "10.0.4.5",
					"uaadomain": "authentication.us20.hana.ondemand.com",
					"onpremise_proxy_port": "20003",
					"verificationkey": "-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\nMYPUBLICKEY\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----",
					"identityzone": "myorg",
					"tenantid": "1234",
					"onpremise_proxy_rfc_port": "20001"
				},
				"syslog_drain_url": null,
				"volume_mounts": []
			}
		],

 

If you look closely, we're redirecting the calls to the onpremise_proxy_port (20003) of the onpremise_proxy_host (10.0.4.5) which is the host:port of the backend configured in the Cloud Connector.

So everytime a request  goes to the local 20003 port, it will redirect - through the ssh tunnel - to 10.0.4.5:20003.

Let me know if you have further questions.

entry.title: Re: Financial Accounting 4 Advanced Execution Service Scenario in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:

entry.title: G2 Leadership for Master Data Management: Our Continued Show of Excellence
entry.content:
In the dynamic world of enterprise data management, achieving excellence based on reviews and rating from our customers is truly a milestone worth celebrating. We are thrilled to announce that we have been recognized as a spring 2025 leader in Master Data Management (MDM) by G2, the world's leading business solutions review platform. We believe the continuous recognition is a testament to our commitment to innovation, customer satisfaction, and continuous improvement.

Customer-Centric Approach

At the heart of our success is our customer-centric approach. We believe that our customers' feedback and insights are invaluable in shaping our products and services. By actively listening to their needs and addressing their pain points, we have been able to deliver solutions that truly make a difference. The positive reviews and high satisfaction ratings on G2 are a direct reflection of our commitment to putting our customers first.  Learn how our Customer Influence programs allow you to influence and adopt SAP innovations throughout the product lifecycle.

Innovation and Adaptability

In a rapidly evolving technological landscape, innovation and adaptability are key. Our team of experts continually explores new trends and technologies, ensuring that our MDM solutions remain at the forefront of the industry. Whether it's leveraging GenAI for automation and efficiency in master data governance or implementing analytics for better data quality management or MDM processes, we are always striving to stay ahead of the curve.  See what‚Äôs new in SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA and cloud edition.

Looking Ahead

While we celebrate this milestone, we remain focused on the future. We are committed to building on this success and continuing to deliver innovative, high-quality MDM solutions that meet the evolving needs of our customers.  To view what is planned and coming next, check out the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

We are grateful to G2 for this recognition and to our customers for their trust and support. Thank you for being a part of our journey. Together, we will continue to redefine the future of Master Data Management.

entry.title: Inconsistent data between table KBLP and COOI and FMIOI
entry.content:
Please find attached document having screen shots of below mentioned issue we have in our production system. 

Issue:

We have found some inconsistency between EARMARKED FUNDS (Funds Commitment) table KBLP and  FM commitment documents FMIOI and CO commitment line item table COOI. 

REFX contract 1271/4521776 assigned to funds commitment 7000033525 and you can see in attached document point 4 where REFX contract look ok. The issue is when we see funds commitment 7000033525 via FMZ3 it shows two line item which is also validated via REFX contract as well as in table KBLP. 

However when we run KSB2 commitment item report for cost centers it shows 5 line items in which item item 1 is ok, item 2 has incorrect amount and item 3, 4 and 5 is showing which not exist in FMZ3 and KBLP. 

KSB2 retrieved commitments amount from table COOI which is commitment details table and this table also contain 5 line items with 3 , 4 and 5 not exist in FMZ3

Also FM commitment document table FMIOI is showing 5 items where as item 1 and 2 are correct and 3, 4 and 5 is in this table however they are not in FMZ3 or in KBLP

Following Requests from SAP:

  1. How can we delete or removed incorrect entries from COOI and FMIOI to make sure our free balance reports , KSB2 and all reports showing only two items exactly the way we seeing in tale KBLP and via FMZ3
  2. Also if SAP can highlight what would be the potential causes which have this inconsistency. 

 

As this is just example of one contract and funds commitments however we have issue in multiple contracts and funds commitment so we highly appreciate if SAP can pinpoint some program, OSS note or any fix which delete those inconsistent document for our commitment reports.

entry.title: Re: Recruiting - Hide the "Create New Job Requisition from Blank Template"
entry.content:
Hello.

I stumbled on this while looking for an answer myself.

Definitely not an ideal solution, but what I've proposed to our client is ..

  1. Remove the Hide Create Requisition permission from the Manager Self Service Roles
  2. Create a Permission Group including everyone except for those in Department = Talent Acquisition, e.g., 'All Employees ex TA'
  3. Create a new Permission Role called 'Hide Create Requisition', with just the one permission in it, and assign that Role to Managers From Group = All Employees ex TA

It does add complexity, but the Permission Role is clearly named and it's easy for anyone to see what the purpose is.

If you found a better solution since you posted I'd love to hear about it.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Assignment Basis at Variable Pay Worksheet
entry.content:
Hi @Olga_Skarla

Is what you wanted to say : "Can we bring the value from the Assignment Basis field into a column of the summary level of Variable Pay" ? 

The answer is that while it is technically possible for a person with only 1 assignment, it will not work correctly for someone with 2 assignments as the system will always aggregate the values from different assignments to show them on the summary level (there is no way to differentiate the basis field from assignment 1 from the basis field from assignment 2 or 3 or 4). And if you simply take the standard ID "basis" and paste in the entry level column it will always show the value of the latest assignment.

On this topic you will find the following blogs insightful I think: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/how-to-build-assignment-data-checks-at-the-summary-level-of-variable-pay/ba-p/13559409 

Also you can look at Option 3 from this blog which explains the pre-requisites to bring standard varpayemphist columns into ALCF to be able to then use them in the entry level : https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/3-must-know-configuration-setups-in-variable-pay/ba-p/13514367

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: ADT Code Completion is Slow
entry.content:
Good Nigth
My Name is Raquel Souza
I'm from Brazil

Yes, this is a common issue in Eclipse with ADT (ABAP Development Tools). SE38 is much faster because it is a native SAP GUI tool, while Eclipse processes more information and depends on specific settings.

Here are some solutions to improve code completion performance in Eclipse with ADT: ### 1.
Adjust Auto Complete Display Time Eclipse allows you to configure the code completion activation time.
To adjust this:
1. Go to Window > Preferences.
2. Navigate to Abap Development > Editors > Source Code Editors > Code Completion.
3. Adjust the Completion Delay to a lower value, such as 50 ms.
4. Uncheck unnecessary options that may be hogging the resource.
### 2. Increase Eclipse Memory If Eclipse is running low on memory,
it may affect performance.
To increase memory:
1. Open the `eclipse.ini` file in the Eclipse installation folder.
2. Modify or add these lines: ``` -Xms512m -Xmx2048m ``` This will increase the memory allocation and may improve performance.

Good luck !

I hope I

entry.title: RW011 Errorin Invoice Verification‚ÄîIs It Really a Problem or Just How It Works?
entry.content:
In procurement scenarios, accounting documents are typically created when we post goods movement (GR) and process invoice verification (IR). It's true that in about 99% of GR and IR postings, an accounting document is generated. So, it's understandable if you feel anxious when the system tells you an accounting document doesn't exist.

Yes, there are indeed cases where the RW011 error might indicate a potential data inconsistency. For example, you might come across scenarios mentioned in the following KBA:

  • 2009994 - Error RW011: No subsequent document found in accounting - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA

Usually, these "real" inconsistencies mentioned in the KBA above, are caused by a rollback due to improper Commit Work or Roll Back in non-standard logic. To correct this, you'll need to run report ZREPMIR7 (from SAP Note 382797).

 

However, there are cases where the RW011 error is not actually a big deal.

Take, for example, when you manually post an invoice via T-code MIRO. If the Purchase Order (PO) referred to by this invoice has the ERS indicator set, and you process the ERS program (T-code MRRL), a zero-amount invoice will be created. Checking the accounting document will trigger this error. Refer to KBA:

  • 1543167 - Invoice is posted with zero amount in T-code MRRL - SAP ERP & S/4 HANA

Another ERS scenario might occur when you post GR for the ERS PO, then reverse this GR (movement type 102) or post a return delivery (movement type 122). This clears the total GR quantity to zero just before running the ERS program. Consequently, running the ERS program results in a zero-amount invoice, with no accounting document generated. See KBA:

  • 2915665 - LIV: Zero Invoice when using MRRL transaction after Goods Receipt reversed - SAP ERP & SAP S/4HANA

 

Or imagine a PO where subsequent debits or credits are posted, making the GR amount equal to the IR amount. Since subsequent debits or credits don't affect the total invoice quantity, processing account maintenance using T-code MR11 will target this PO. As a result, the account maintenance document won't have a follow-on accounting document because it doesn't change any FI account amounts. Refer to KBA:

  • 2382993 - MR11: No FI document for Account Maintenance - SAP ERP & SAP S/4 HANA

 

So, now that you've reached this point, this error doesn't seem as alarming anymore, right? In conclusion, when faced with this error, first take a deep breath, calm down, and check the PO history to see if the document was meant to be a zero-amount document. If it was, no worries‚Äîthe accounting document shouldn't exist. (It would actually be concerning if an accounting document appeared in this case, LOL)

If the document isn‚Äôt supposed to be zero-amount, don‚Äôt fret‚Äîthe solution is still straightforward. Report ZREPMIR7 will help identify all potential inconsistencies, setting the invoice document status to "On hold" so you can decide its fate (delete or repost it).

entry.title: Re: SAP RE-FX intercompany lease ?
entry.content:
In that case you should request access to your reference client 000 where you can see all the pre-delivered SAP reference configuration. If you get them to create an RFC in your config client to point to 000 you will be able to simply select and import the missing config entries that you would like to copy over to your config client.

Very likely what has happened is that you are setting up RE-FX and an existing system where RE-FX has never been activated or configured during the initial implementation. In that case there will be not config data at all in your config client and you'll have an empty RE-FX system.

I highly recommend you don't start from scratch but use client 000 as a reference.

Regards, Andre

SAP Analytics Cloud„ÅÆ„Ç´„É¨„É≥„ÉÄÊ©üËÉΩ„Åß„ÅØ„ÄÅ„Çø„Çπ„ÇØ„Å®„Éó„É≠„Çª„Çπ„Çí‰ΩøÁî®„Åó„Å¶„ÉØ„Éº„ÇØ„Éï„É≠„Éº„ÅÆ‰ΩúÊàê„ÇÑ„Ç∏„Éß„Éñ„ÅÆÁÆ°ÁêÜ„ÇíË°å„ÅÜ„Åì„Å®„Åå„Åß„Åç„Åæ„Åô„ÄÇ‰æã„Åà„Å∞„ÄÅ‰∫àÁÆó„ÅÆÂÖ•Âäõ„ÇÑÊâøË™ç„ÄÅ„Éá„Éº„Çø„ÅÆ„É≠„ÉÉ„ÇØ„ÇÑÈÖçË≥¶Âá¶ÁêÜ„Å™„Å©‰∫àÁÆóÁ∑®Êàê„ÇÑ‰∫àÁÆóÁ≠ñÂÆö„Å´Èñ¢„Çè„Çã„Çø„Çπ„ÇØ„Çí„Ç´„É¨„É≥„ÉÄ„Éº„Éô„Éº„Çπ„ÅßÁôªÈå≤„Åô„Çã„Åì„Å®„Åå„Åß„Åç„ÄÅÈÄ≤ÊçóÁä∂Ê≥Å„Çí„É¢„Éã„Çø„É™„É≥„Ç∞„Åô„Çã„Åì„Å®„Åå„Åß„Åç„Åæ„Åô„ÄÇ

„Çø„Çπ„ÇØ„ÅÆÁ®ÆÈ°û

entry.title: Joule Capability Questions
entry.content:
I am working with a client who is evaluating SAP Joule Private Edition and would like to confirm whether it supports specific query and analysis capabilities across different SAP modules. Could you help clarify whether Joule can handle the following use cases?

1. Sales & Distribution (SD) Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Delivery Note (DN) details?

  • Can Joule query Sales Orders (SO) from a Customer's perspective? (For example: Tell me the due deliver sales order for customer ABCDE)

2. Materials Management (MM) Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Goods Receipt (GR) data?

  • Can Joule query Purchasing Orders (PO) from a Vendor's perspective? (For example: Tell me the due deliver purchasing order for vendor ABCDE)

3. Production Planning (PP) Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Goods Issue (GI) data for a Manufacturing Order (MO)?

  • Can Joule generate a Manufacturing Order (MO) Shortage Report?

4. Finance & Controlling (FICO) Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Overdue Accounts Receivable (AR)?

  • Can Joule convert data dimensions and aggregate (e.g., Customer ‚Üí Customer Group ‚Üí BUBG)(For example: Tell me the overdue AR percentage of a customer / customer group / BU)

  • Can Joule retrieve Credit Exposure details, specifically Credit Line Balance?

  • Can Joule retrieve Overdue Accounts Payable (AP) for Vendors?

  • Can Joule generate a Margin Report?

5. Business Partner Capabilities

  • Can Joule retrieve Payment Terms?

  • Can Joule retrieve Incoterms?

Joule

entry.title: SAP Opens Restaurant
entry.content:
Theis fine day, 01/04/2025, SAP is opening a new restaurant. It is called the ‚ÄúAI Powered Quantum Computing Restaurant Unleashed in the Restaurant Data Cloud‚Äù. It is located at the end of the universe, which is quite appropriate as that is what many customers feel has happened to them after going live with SAP.

Upon arrival you get given a complimentary can of the latest SAP beer, which is flavoured with lemon and lime, it will get your one, two, three, four five, senses working overtime.

 

This dining establishment it going to be very famous for its beer, but it also has a ‚ÄúJoule‚Äù purpose if you see what AI mean. That is, as might be expected it serves food. There is only one item on the menu, roasted seaweed, but it can customized as much as you want, so much so that even ‚Äúcitizen developers‚Äù can alter the nature of the meal by going into the kitchen and taking over the role you would normally expect a chef to do.

Here we see the food in its basic form of roasted seaweed.

 

As you can see it is called OBAP which by a wonderful co-incidence is also the name of SAP‚Äôs propriety programming language.

I even wrote a book about that language and where it was headed and have published several updated editions every few years. That book was called ‚ÄúOBAP to the Future‚Äù.

 

You can see the Death Star at the bottom right of my book cover. I had to get that in somehow, as Darth Vader was an OBAP programmer, and Yoda designed some elements of the OBAP programming language, which is why you see statements like IF NOT MONSTER_SIZE IS INITIAL.

Going back to the dining experience, after your INITIAL free beer, with your roasted seaweed dish you can have a choice of three different beers (pictured) all made in the ‚Äú51st State‚Äù as Donald Trump calls it.

 

 

I have been assured that is not how SAP view their customers, no, no, not at all. Though they did become the most valuable company in Europe the other day, so they are getting their money from somewhere.

Anyway, I look forward to seeing all of you who remain the SAP Community down in the new restaurant for a beer and seaweed meal. No need to make a reservation, I think we can all easily fit inside, even if the place only has one table. The original design had lots of tables, but that was simplified down to one.

See you there!

Cheersy Cheers

Paul

entry.title: Discover the Updates with Event-Based Revenue Recognition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition continues to evolve, bringing new features and enhancements that streamline business processes and improve financial reporting. Staying updated with these changes is crucial for businesses to leverage the full potential of their ERP system. In this blog, we will delve into two significant updates in the 2502.2 release within the Event-Based Revenue Recognition module of Revenue and Cost Accounting: the new item category for service orders and the changed custom source usage.

New Item Category for Service Orders

Overview: The latest release introduces a new item category, SVB7 Service Bundle (Ad Hoc Billing), designed to support ad hoc billing plans for service orders with Event-Based Revenue Recognition. This addition is a game-changer for businesses that require flexible billing options.

Business Details: SVB7 allows businesses to use Service Bundle in ad hoc billing plans, which are essential for managing service orders with event-based revenue recognition. This new item category is applicable for revenue recognition keys CCOCC and CCOFC, ensuring accurate and timely revenue recognition.

Technical Details: The introduction of SVB7 impacts several scope items, including 3M3 (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents), 3M4 (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents IFRS), and 3M5 (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Service Documents - US GAAP). 

Expert Insight: Using SVB7 for service orders offers numerous benefits, such as enhanced flexibility in billing and improved accuracy in revenue recognition. This update can significantly streamline business processes and ensure compliance with financial reporting standards.

Changed Custom Source Usage in Event-Based Revenue Recognition

Overview: The 2502.2 release also brings changes to custom source usage in event-based revenue recognition. The configuration activity Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition has been simplified, narrowing down to a single account assignment rule, COSPTM. It's used for time and material scenarios.

Business Details: Use custom sources if you want to split recognized revenue by posting to different accounts. Previously, assignment rule usages 400 and 401 were required, but with this update, they are no longer necessary. This change is particularly relevant for the configuration activity Maintain Settings for Event-Based Revenue Recognition. 

Technical Details: The affected scope items include 1IL (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Project-Based Services), 1P0 (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Project-Based Services IFRS), 33O (Event-Based Revenue Recognition - Project-Based Services US GAAP), and several others. To learn more about custom sources, see 3537769.

Expert Insight: Simplifying custom source usage by narrowing down to COSPTM offers several advantages. It reduces complexity in configuration and implementation processes, making it easier for businesses to maintain accurate revenue recognition settings.

Conclusion

The latest updates in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502.2 bring valuable enhancements that can significantly improve business operations. The new item category for service orders and the changed custom source usage in event-based revenue recognition offer greater flexibility and simplicity. Businesses should explore these features to leverage their benefits fully and stay ahead in the competitive landscape.

entry.title: Dynamic MTD, QTD, YTD for Current and Previous Year in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Story
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a requirement to build a report in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) that displays Month-to-Date (MTD), Quarter-to-Date (QTD), and Year-to-Date (YTD) values for both the current and previous year. The user should be able to select a month, quarter, or year using a drop-down Story input control, and the report should dynamically show the corresponding data for both the current and previous years.

The story is built on top of a Datasphere Analytical Model (AM), and I have created several calculated measures (e.g., Net Sales, COGS). I am also using a date dimension, but currently, I need to manually select dates from both the current and previous years. Additionally, the date dimension is displayed in a hierarchy format, which requires drilling down after selecting a quarter or month, and this results in extra columns that make the report cluttered.

Is there a way to automatically fetch the previous year's data when the user selects a date for the current year, so I can avoid selecting dates for both years and eliminate the extra columns and the need to drill down?

Any suggestions or best practices for implementing this would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Process Order Not creating PMR in EWM 950
entry.content:
Hi @Dhood ,

Did you check Production Scheduling ? Remember it should work for process order also. 

Please set up WM Request in Production Scheduling, then release process order and let us know if further questions...

Please refer to OSS note - 

3133948 - How to trigger the automatic EWM staging upon the production order release

Resolution

Since the "Posting Change Item" is a kind of "Delivery", if you want to trigger automatic EWM staging, you have to set the "WM Request" field of the Production Scheduling Profile as "2 - Transfer Reqmt, Transfer Order, and Delivery Upon Release".

Community Member is already answered: https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/how-to-set-up-pmr-automatically-when-production-order-is-released/qaq-p/13799579 

 

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Issue with Updating Columns and Hierarchies in SAC Story from Datasphere Analytical Model
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I‚Äôve created a story in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) based on an Analytical Model (AM) in Datasphere. Initially, the model had 10 columns and 2 hierarchies. Later, I added a new column and hierarchy to the model, but these updates are not reflected in the SAC story.

As a temporary solution, I created a new AM and built a new story, but this doesn‚Äôt seem like a sustainable option in the long run, especially when frequent updates to the model are required.

Is this a known limitation of the Analytical Model, or is there a more efficient way to ensure that updates to the AM are automatically reflected in the SAC story without needing to recreate everything from scratch?

Any advice or suggestions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!
